meta.title: Apostolic Succession and The Papacy | Protesting or Catholic Ep 2
meta.author: Canon Press

questions.question: According to the material, what is the singular thing that makes someone Roman Catholic or not?
options: Accepting the canon of Scripture
Belief in the apostolic authority of the papacy
Belief in the real presence in the Eucharist
Submission to the Council of Trent
questions.answer: Belief in the apostolic authority of the papacy

questions.question: Why does the material say that if any one of the four underlying doctrines is wrong, the entire papal project collapses?
options: Because the doctrines were defined at the same council
Because the claims are interdependent; each builds on the others
Because Protestants only attack one at a time
Because Scripture explicitly links all four
questions.answer: Because the claims are interdependent; each builds on the others

questions.question: How do Protestants generally understand apostolic succession, in contrast to the Catholic view?
options: As a succession of bishops in Rome only
As apostolic succession of doctrine—the apostolic teaching of the gospel in Scripture
As a succession of miracles and signs
As a succession of church councils
questions.answer: As apostolic succession of doctrine—the apostolic teaching of the gospel in Scripture

questions.question: In the New Testament and earliest Christian writings, how are episcopos (bishop/overseer) and presbyteros (elder) related?
options: Bishop is always superior to elder
They are used interchangeably; they refer to the same office
Elder is the Greek term for what Latin calls bishop
Presbyteros refers only to the Twelve Apostles
questions.answer: They are used interchangeably; they refer to the same office

questions.question: Which passage shows that Timothy was ordained by the college of elders rather than by a single bishop?
options: Acts 20
Titus 1:5–7
1 Timothy 4:14
Philippians 1:1
questions.answer: 1 Timothy 4:14

questions.question: According to most scholarship cited in the material, when did a single bishop of Rome and the distinct office of bishop take shape?
options: During the lifetime of the apostles
The latter half of the 2nd century
At the Council of Nicaea
At the Council of Trent
questions.answer: The latter half of the 2nd century

questions.question: St. Jerome is cited as saying that the bishop originally derived his authority from what?
options: Direct appointment by an apostle
The choice of the presbyters
A vision from Christ
Imperial decree
questions.answer: The choice of the presbyters

questions.question: At Alexandria, from Mark the Evangelist until Heraclas and Dionysius, how was the bishop chosen?
options: By the apostle's successor in Rome
By divine lot
The presbyters named one of their own number as bishop, chosen by themselves
By the emperor
questions.answer: The presbyters named one of their own number as bishop, chosen by themselves

questions.question: What does the material conclude about a system of bishops as church government?
options: It is forbidden by Scripture
It can be consistent with Scripture as prudent church government, but claims to a special exclusive succession of bishops are on shaky ground
It is required by the Council of Nicaea
Only the bishop of Rome may claim succession
questions.answer: It can be consistent with Scripture as prudent church government, but claims to a special exclusive succession of bishops are on shaky ground

questions.question: Who presided over the Jerusalem Council in Acts?
options: Peter
Paul
James
John
questions.answer: James

questions.question: In Matthew 18, who is given the authority to bind and loose, similar to what Peter is given elsewhere?
options: Only Peter's successors
The rest of the disciples as well
Only the Twelve
Only bishops
questions.answer: The rest of the disciples as well

questions.question: What did Cyprian of Carthage say about the other apostles in relation to Peter?
options: They were subject to Peter in all matters
They were what Peter was, furnished with an equal share of honor and power
They received their authority from Peter
They were inferior to Peter in teaching
questions.answer: They were what Peter was, furnished with an equal share of honor and power

questions.question: What did the 1980s Anglican–Catholic ecumenical dialogue acknowledge about the New Testament and the primacy of the bishop of Rome?
options: That the New Testament explicitly establishes the Roman primacy
That the New Testament offers no sufficient basis for it
That only Catholics may interpret the Petrine texts
That the dialogue was inconclusive
questions.answer: That the New Testament offers no sufficient basis for it

questions.question: According to Francis Turretin, in what sense do bishops and elders succeed the apostles?
options: In the extraordinary apostolic office, including revelation and judging the twelve tribes
Only in the bishop of Rome
Only in ordinary ministry (preaching, sacraments, discipline), not in the extraordinary apostolic role
In exactly the same way the Twelve succeeded Christ
questions.answer: Only in ordinary ministry (preaching, sacraments, discipline), not in the extraordinary apostolic role

questions.question: Which two patriarchates besides Rome are said to have their own claims of Petrine succession?
options: Constantinople and Jerusalem
Antioch and Alexandria
Ephesus and Carthage
Corinth and Thessalonica
questions.answer: Antioch and Alexandria

questions.question: What does the material say chiefly distinguished Rome in the early church?
options: A unique divine mandate recorded in Scripture
Largely secular and political status as cultural center and capital of the empire
The earliest liturgy
The only see with apostolic graves
questions.answer: Largely secular and political status as cultural center and capital of the empire

questions.question: What is Roman primacy, as defined in the material?
options: The belief that Rome is the capital of the church
The combination of apostolic succession and Petrine primacy applied to the bishop of Rome
The belief that all bishops are equal
The doctrine of papal infallibility
questions.answer: The combination of apostolic succession and Petrine primacy applied to the bishop of Rome

questions.question: In the Catholic view, what does valid ministry depend on?
options: Faithfulness to Scripture alone
Whether bishops can trace their ordination back to the apostles
Recognition by the state
The consent of the laity
questions.answer: Whether bishops can trace their ordination back to the apostles

questions.question: First Clement (traditionally 96 AD, from Rome) is used as evidence for what?
options: The three-office hierarchy from the start
The same two-office (elder/bishop) model as the New Testament
Petrine primacy in Rome
The necessity of a single bishop per city
questions.answer: The same two-office (elder/bishop) model as the New Testament

questions.question: Why does the material say that the church fathers did not agree on what "this rock" referred to in Jesus's words to Peter?
options: The text was corrupted early on
They agreed it was Peter only
They offered differing interpretations—Peter himself, Peter's faith, or Jesus
They did not comment on the passage
questions.answer: They offered differing interpretations—Peter himself, Peter's faith, or Jesus

questions.question: According to the material, what should we believe St. Jerome about bishops?
options: That they were established by Christ at the Last Supper
That they were created as a practical organizational measure, not as the foundation of church legitimacy and authority
That only the bishop of Rome has apostolic authority
That they replace the need for Scripture
questions.answer: That they were created as a practical organizational measure, not as the foundation of church legitimacy and authority

questions.question: The hierarchy visible by the time of the Council of Nicaea is described in the material as what?
options: Clearly mandated in the Gospels
A product of divine command
A product of human custom, not divine command
Defined for the first time at Nicaea
questions.answer: A product of human custom, not divine command

questions.question: Which book records Paul rebuking Peter publicly for a significant error?
options: Acts
Romans
Galatians
1 Corinthians
questions.answer: Galatians

questions.question: What is the three-office view of church government?
options: Elders, deacons, and laity
Bishops, priests, and deacons as distinct divinely instituted offices
Apostles, prophets, and teachers
Pope, cardinals, and bishops
questions.answer: Bishops, priests, and deacons as distinct divinely instituted offices

questions.question: Jerome compared the bishop's relationship to the presbyters to what?
options: A king to his subjects
An apostle to the Twelve
An archdeacon to the deacons
A judge to the court
questions.answer: An archdeacon to the deacons
